revision.text:
'''樽前山神社'''（たるまえやまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *大山津見神・久久能智神・鹿屋野比賣神
==由緒== 創建年代については不明であるが、[[1875年]]（明治8年）に郷社、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に県社に列格した。
==所在地== *北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社]
[[category:北海道の神社|たるまえやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''樽前山神社'''（たるまえやまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *大山津見神・久久能智神・鹿屋野比賣神
==由緒== 創建年代については不明であるが、[[1875年]]（明治8年）に郷社、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に県社に列格した。
==所在地== *北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）]
[[category:北海道の神社|たるまえやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''樽前山神社'''（たるまえやまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *大山津見神・久久能智神・鹿屋野比賣神
==由緒== 創建年代については不明であるが、[[1875年]]（明治8年）に郷社、[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に県社に列格した。
==所在地== *北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）]
[[category:北海道の神社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:府県社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|たるまえやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=樽前山神社| 所在地=北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49| 祭神=大山津見神
久久能智神
鹿屋野比賣神| 創建=明治初年| 社格=県社・別表神社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月15日]]| 神事=山開祭・山納祭}} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえやまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]を祀る。 ==由緒== 明治初年、[[樽前山]]周辺の原野を開拓するに当たり、樽前山山麓に山の神・大山津見神を祀ったのに始まる。明治8年（[[1875年]]）、樹木の神・久久能智神、草原の神・鹿屋野比賣神を合祀し、苫小牧の町の中心部に社殿を造営して遷座、[[郷社]]に列し、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白走郡|白走]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となった。昭和11年（[[1936年]]）に県社に昇格した。昭和61年（[[1986年]]）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 ==施設== 樽前山山頂に奥宮があり、[[6月12日]]に山開祭、[[10月12日]]に山納祭が行われる。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] [[category:北海道の神社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:府県社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|たるまえやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=樽前山神社| 所在地=北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49| 祭神=大山津見神
久久能智神
鹿屋野比賣神| 創建=明治初年| 社格=県社・別表神社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月15日]]| 神事=山開祭・山納祭}} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえやまじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]を祀る。 ==由緒== 明治初年、[[樽前山]]周辺の原野を開拓するに当たり、樽前山山麓に山の神・大山津見神を祀ったのに始まる。明治8年（[[1875年]]）、樹木の神・久久能智神、草原の神・鹿屋野比賣神を合祀し、苫小牧の町の中心部に社殿を造営して遷座、[[郷社]]に列し、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白走郡|白走]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となった。昭和11年（[[1936年]]）に県社に昇格した。昭和61年（[[1986年]]）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 ==施設== 樽前山山頂に奥宮があり、[[6月12日]]に山開祭、[[10月12日]]に山納祭が行われる。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] [[category:北海道の神社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:府県社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|たるまえやましんしや]] [[Category:苫小牧市|たるまえやましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

久久能智神

revision.comment: 神社辞典、神道事典では「たるまえさん」

{{神社| 名称=樽前山神社| 所在地=北海道苫小牧市字高丘6-49| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=大山津見神
久久能智神
鹿屋野比賣神| 創建=明治初年| 社格=県社・別表神社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[7月15日]]| 神事=山開祭・山納祭}} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえざんじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]を祀る。 ==由緒== 明治初年、[[樽前山]]周辺の原野を開拓するに当たり、樽前山山麓に山の神・大山津見神を祀ったのに始まる。明治8年（[[1875年]]）、樹木の神・久久能智神、草原の神・鹿屋野比賣神を合祀し、樽前村から苫小牧の町の中心部に遷座、[[郷社]]に列し、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白老郡|白老]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となった。昭和11年（[[1936年]]）に県社に昇格した。昭和61年（[[1986年]]）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 ==施設== 樽前山山頂に奥宮があり、[[6月12日]]に山開祭、[[10月12日]]に山納祭が行われる。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] *[http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html 樽前山神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:たるまえざんしんしや}} [[category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:苫小牧市]] {{shinto-stub}}

鹿屋野比賣神 |創建=明治初年 |社格=県社・別表神社 |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[7月15日]] |神事=山開祭・山納祭 }} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえざんじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 [[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]を祀る。 ==由緒== 明治初年、[[樽前山]]周辺の原野を開拓するに当たり、樽前山山麓に山の神・大山津見神を祀ったのに始まる。明治8年（[[1875年]]）、樹木の神・久久能智神、草原の神・鹿屋野比賣神を合祀し、樽前村から苫小牧の町の中心部に遷座、[[郷社]]に列し、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白老郡|白老]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となった。昭和11年（[[1936年]]）に県社に昇格した。昭和61年（[[1986年]]）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 ==施設== 樽前山山頂に奥宮があり、[[6月12日]]に山開祭、[[10月12日]]に山納祭が行われる。 === 境内外末社 === * [[聖徳神社]]{{ndash}}[[聖徳太子]]。建築土木の祖神 * [[宇迦之御魂神樽前天満宮]]{{ndash}}[[菅原道真]] * [[苫小牧稲荷神社]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html 樽前山神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:たるまえさんしんしや}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:苫小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:胆振管内]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=北海道}}

{{神社 |名称=樽前山神社 |画像=[[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|250px]]

久久能智神
鹿屋野比賣神 |創建=明治初年 |社格=県社・別表神社 |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[7月15日]] |神事=山開祭・山納祭 }} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえざんじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 [[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]・[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]・[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]を祀る。 ==由緒== 明治初年、[[樽前山]]周辺の原野を開拓するに当たり、樽前山山麓に山の神・大山津見神を祀ったのに始まる。明治8年（[[1875年]]）、樹木の神・久久能智神、草原の神・鹿屋野比賣神を合祀し、樽前村から苫小牧の町の中心部に遷座、[[郷社]]に列し、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白老郡|白老]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となった。昭和11年（[[1936年]]）に県社に昇格した。昭和61年（[[1986年]]）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 ==施設== 樽前山山頂に奥宮があり、[[6月12日]]に山開祭、[[10月12日]]に山納祭が行われる。 === 境内外末社 === * [[聖徳神社]]{{ndash}}[[聖徳太子]]。建築土木の祖神 * 樽前天満宮{{ndash}}[[菅原道真]] * [[苫小牧稲荷神社]] - 宇迦之御魂神 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/public_html/data/all/12008.html 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html 樽前山神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:たるまえさんしんしや}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:苫小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:胆振管内]] [[Category:別表神社]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=北海道}}

It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]]. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}'

revision.comment: 苫小牧市史に基づき改稿、画像を提供
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=樽前山神社 |画像=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
樽前山神社 大鳥居 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地=[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]字高丘6-49 |位置={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[大山津見神]]
[[久久能智神]]
[[鹿屋野比売神]] |創建=不詳 |社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[7月15日]] |神事=山開祭・山納祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=樽前山
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえざんじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ;[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） :山を司る神。 ;[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]（くくのちのかみ） :木々を司る神。 ;[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]（かやのひめのかみ） :原野を司る神。 == 歴史 == 『樽前山神社御由緒略記』によると、当社の前身となる神祠は江戸時代から[[樽前山]]山麓にて祀られており、手水鉢に残る「[[元治]]二年」（[[1865年]]）の年号をその証として挙げている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|pp=819 - 820}}。しかし『苫小牧市史』では、[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に起きた「鯉のぼり大火」によって証拠文書がすべて焼失しているため、件の手水鉢が樽前山山麓から移されたとする根拠がないと指摘されている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=820}}。 『苫小牧町史』には出典が不明ながらも、[[1874年]]（明治7年）7月12日に樽前山神社を苫細駅西側に建立する旨の願出があり、許可を得たとの記載がある{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=819}}。 [[1875年]]（明治8年）5月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]通達により郷社として定められる{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=819}}。『樽前山神社御由緒略記』ではこのとき、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白老郡|白老]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となったとしているが、『苫小牧市史』によると千歳郡と白老郡の郷社はそれぞれの稲荷神社であり、樽前山神社が「三郡総鎮守」というのは誤りとしている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|pp=820, 822}}。いずれにせよ、前述の大火で資料が失われているため、樽前山神社の由緒については不明点が多い{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=822}}。 1921年（大正10年）の大火後、矢代町の社地に移転して、再造営を行う{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。[[1923年]]（大正12年）11月、正遷宮式を挙行{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。 [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、県社に昇格した{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。 [[1975年]]（昭和50年）に鎮座100年を迎え、その記念事業として高丘への移転計画が立てられる{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1244}}。 [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）3月15日に高丘で地鎮祭が行われて本格的な工事が始まり、同年10月8日には本殿の上棟式が行われた{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1244}}。そして[[1992年]]（平成4年）7月14日、樽前山神社遷座奉祝大祭が挙行され、矢代町の旧社殿から高丘の新社殿へと神体が移された{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|pp=1244 - 1245}}。 == 施設 == ;天満宮 :[[菅原道真]]を祀る境内社。 ;稲荷大明神 :宇迦之御魂神を祀る境内社。 ;聖徳神社 :[[聖徳太子]]を祀る境内社。 :[[1927年]]（昭和2年）10月1日、幸町に建てられた太子堂を始まりとする{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1247}}。 :[[1996年]]（平成8年）9月、高丘の樽前山神社敷地内に移転した{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1248}}。 ;奥宮 :樽前山山頂にある。 File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|天満宮 File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|稲荷大明神 File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|聖徳神社 File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|奥宮 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|title=苫小牧市史|volume=下巻|date=1976-03-31|publisher=苫小牧市|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|苫小牧市史 下|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|和書|title=苫小牧市史|volume=追補編|date=2001-03-25|publisher=苫小牧市|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|苫小牧市史 追補|2001}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html 樽前山神社 公式サイト] {{DEFAULTSORT:たるまえさんしんしや}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:苫小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:胆振管内]] [[Category:別表神社]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== {{Shrine |Name = Tarumaezan Shrine |Image = [[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = [[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49 |Coordinates = {{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]] |Founded = Unknown |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine |Main Hall= [[Nagare-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = July 15 |Rites = Yamabiraki Festival, Yamanome Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : Deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : Deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : Deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : Established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

[[鹿屋野比売神]] |創建=不詳 |社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |本殿=[[流造]] |例祭=[[7月15日]] |神事=山開祭・山納祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=樽前山
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''樽前山神社'''（たるまえざんじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[苫小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == ;[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]]（おおやまつみのかみ） :山を司る神。 ;[[ククノチ|久久能智神]]（くくのちのかみ） :木々を司る神。 ;[[カヤノヒメ|鹿屋野比賣神]]（かやのひめのかみ） :原野を司る神。 == 歴史 == 『樽前山神社御由緒略記』によると、当社の前身となる神祠は江戸時代から[[樽前山]]山麓にて祀られており、手水鉢に残る「[[元治]]二年」（[[1865年]]）の年号をその証として挙げている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|pp=819 - 820}}。しかし『苫小牧市史』では、[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に起きた「鯉のぼり大火」によって証拠文書がすべて焼失しているため、件の手水鉢が樽前山山麓から移されたとする根拠がないと指摘されている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=820}}。 『苫小牧町史』には出典が不明ながらも、[[1874年]]（明治7年）7月12日に樽前山神社を苫細駅西側に建立する旨の願出があり、許可を得たとの記載がある{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=819}}。 [[1875年]]（明治8年）5月、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]通達により郷社として定められる{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=819}}。『樽前山神社御由緒略記』ではこのとき、[[千歳郡|千歳]]・[[白老郡|白老]]（しらおい）・[[勇払郡|勇払]]（ゆうふつ）三郡の総鎮守となったとしているが、『苫小牧市史』によると千歳郡と白老郡の郷社はそれぞれの稲荷神社であり、樽前山神社が「三郡総鎮守」というのは誤りとしている{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|pp=820, 822}}。いずれにせよ、前述の大火で資料が失われているため、樽前山神社の由緒については不明点が多い{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=822}}。 1921年（大正10年）の大火後、矢代町の社地に移転して、再造営を行う{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。[[1923年]]（大正12年）11月、正遷宮式を挙行{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。 [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、県社に昇格した{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 下|1976|p=823}}。 [[1975年]]（昭和50年）に鎮座100年を迎え、その記念事業として高丘への移転計画が立てられる{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1244}}。 [[1986年]]（昭和61年）、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列された。 [[1991年]]（[[平成]]3年）3月15日に高丘で地鎮祭が行われて本格的な工事が始まり、同年10月8日には本殿の上棟式が行われた{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1244}}。そして[[1992年]]（平成4年）7月14日、樽前山神社遷座奉祝大祭が挙行され、矢代町の旧社殿から高丘の新社殿へと神体が移された{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|pp=1244 - 1245}}。 == 施設 == ;天満宮 :[[菅原道真]]を祀る境内社。 ;稲荷大明神 :宇迦之御魂神を祀る境内社。 ;聖徳神社 :[[聖徳太子]]を祀る境内社。 :[[1927年]]（昭和2年）10月1日、幸町に建てられた太子堂を始まりとする{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1247}}。 :[[1996年]]（平成8年）9月、高丘の樽前山神社敷地内に移転した{{Sfn|苫小牧市史 追補|2001|p=1248}}。 ;奥宮 :樽前山山頂にある。 File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|天満宮 File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|稲荷大明神 File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|聖徳神社 File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|奥宮 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|title=苫小牧市史|volume=下巻|date=1976-03-31|publisher=苫小牧市|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|苫小牧市史 下|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|和書|title=苫小牧市史|volume=追補編|date=2001-03-25|publisher=苫小牧市|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|苫小牧市史 追補|2001}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 樽前山神社（北海道神社庁）] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html 樽前山神社 公式サイト] {{DEFAULTSORT:たるまえさんしんしや}} [[Category:北海道の神社]] [[Category:苫小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の旧県社]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:胆振管内]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== {{Shrine |Name = Tarumaezan Shrine |Image = [[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = [[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49 |Coordinates = {{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]] |Founded = Unknown |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine |Main Hall= [[Nagare-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = July 15 |Rites = Yamabiraki Festival, Yamanome Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : Deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : Deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : Deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : Established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : Established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== {{Shrine |name = Tarumaezan Shrine |image = [[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location = [[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49 |coordinates = {{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]] |founded = Unknown |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine |Main Hall= [[Nagare-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = July 15 |Rites = Yamabiraki Festival, Yamanome Festival |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tarumaezan Shrine |image = [[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location = [[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49 |coordinates = {{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]] |founded = Unknown |rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine |main hall= [[Nagare-zukuri]] |annual festival = July 15 |Rites = Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival |map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|Deities=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]

[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|annual festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} [[ja:樽前山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|Deities=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|Deities=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia31]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia31]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia23]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} [[ja:樽前山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|Deities=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tarumaezan Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]]{{Japan-religious-struct-stub}}

[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae.
