History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]]

[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:胆振国]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:苫小牧市の建築物]]

{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tomakomai]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]]

three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. {{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[Hokkaido]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[Iburi Subprefecture]], [[Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Iburi Subprefecture|Iburi Subprefecture]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:苫小牧の Municipal History]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Iburi Subprefecture|Iburi Subprefecture]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Tomakomai, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unsourced|date=January 2022}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation=[[Shinto]]|name=Tarumaezan Shrine|image=[[File:Tarumaezan Shrine - Gate.jpg|270px]]
Tarumaezan Shrine Grand Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}}|location=[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]][[Tomakomai]] Aza Takaoka 6-49|coordinates={{coord|42|39|39.19|N|141|36|14.79|E|scale:20000_region:JP|display=inline,title}}|deity=[[Ōyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]]
[[Kukunochi no Kami|Kukunochi no Kami]]
[[Kayano-hime no Kami|Kayano-hime no Kami]]|founded=Unknown|Rank=Former Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine|main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]]|festival=July 15|Rites=Yamabiraki festival, Yamanome festival|map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=39|lat_sec=39.1|lon_deg=141|lon_min=36|lon_sec=14.7|label=Tarumaezan
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}}}}''' [[File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|thumb|]] '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (樽前山神社, ''Tarumaezan Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Iburi Subprefecture|Iburi Subprefecture]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is located on [[Mount Tarumae]], and it was promoted to a prefectural shrine in 1936. It enshrines the Shinto [[kami]] Kukunochi (久久能智神), Kaya no hime (鹿屋野比賣神), and Oyamatsumi (大山津見神). ==Overview== '''Tarumaezan Shrine''' (たるまえざんじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tomakomai]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is a former prefectural shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ;[[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]] : deity governing mountains. ;[[Kukunochi no Kami]] : deity governing trees. ;[[Kayano-hime no Kami]] : deity governing the plains. == History == According to the 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records', the predecessor of the shrine was a shrine located at the base of Mount Tarumae during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with a water basin inscribed with the year "Genji 2" (1865) cited as evidence.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=819-820}} However, 'Tomakomai City History' notes that a large fire in 1921, known as the "Koinobori Fire", destroyed all documentary evidence, casting doubt on the claim that the water basin was moved from the base of Mount Tarumae.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=820}} The 'Tomakomai Town History' mentions, although the source is unclear, that on July 12, 1874, there was a petition to establish Tarumaezan Shrine on the west side of Tomosai Station, which was approved.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} In May 1875, the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha|village shrine]] by the [[:en:Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Home Affairs]].{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=819}} The 'Tarumaezan Shrine Historical Records' state that it became the general guardian shrine for the three districts of Chitose, Shiraoi, and Yūfutsu, but 'Tomakomai City History' clarifies that the village shrines for Chitose and Shiraoi districts were their respective Inari shrines, thus correcting the claim of being the "general guardian shrine for three districts".{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|pp=820, 822}} Regardless, many aspects of the shrine's history remain unclear due to the loss of records in the aforementioned fire.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=822}} After the large fire in 1921, the shrine was relocated to Yashiro Town and rebuilt.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} The official relocation ceremony took place in November 1923.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1936, the shrine was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976|p=823}} In 1975, the shrine marked its 100th anniversary, leading to plans to relocate to Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} In 1986, it was designated as a Beppyo Shrine under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. On March 15, 1991, a ground-breaking ceremony was held at Takaoka, marking the start of full-scale construction, with the main hall's framework completed on October 8 of the same year.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1244}} On July 14, 1992, the relocation ceremony was held, transferring the deities from the old shrine building in Yashiro Town to the new shrine building in Takaoka.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|pp=1244-1245}} == Facilities == ; Tenmangu Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Inari Daimyojin Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[Ukanomitama no Kami]]. ; Shotoku Shrine : An auxiliary shrine dedicated to [[:en:Prince Shotoku|Prince Shotoku]]. : established on October 1, 1927, as a Taishido (Prince Shotoku Hall) in Saiwaicho.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1247}} : Relocated to the Tarumaezan Shrine grounds in Takaoka in September 1996.{{Sfn|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001|p=1248}} ; Okumiya Shrine : Located at the summit of Mount Tarumae. File:Tenman-gū in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Tenmangu Shrine File:Inari Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Inari Daimyojin Shrine File:Shotoku Shrine in Tarumaezan Shrine.jpg|Shotoku Shrine File:MtTarumae-Jin Ja-Okumiya.JPG|Okumiya Shrine == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Vol. 2|date=1976-03-31|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Vol. 2|1976}}}} *{{Cite book|title=Tomakomai City History|volume=Supplement|date=2001-03-25|publisher=Tomakomai City|ref={{SfnRef|Tomakomai City History Supplement|2001}}}} == External Links == * [https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/%e6%a8%bd%e5%89%8d%e5%b1%b1%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Tarumaezan Shrine (Hokkaido Shrine Association)] * [http://www.tarumaesanjinja.com/index.html Tarumaezan Shrine Official Site] ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[https://www.tarumaesanjinja.net Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.66098|141.60447|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} ]] {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|樽前山神社|version=103699982|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:樽前山神社]] [[en:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[ja:Tarumaezan Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Iburi Province]] [[Category:Iburi Subprefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Tomakomai]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomakomai]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]] {{Commons category|Tarumaezan-jinja}} [[mr:तरुमेझान तीर्थ]]
